source.title: Consulate defensive setups across all four sites (CEO/Meeting, Basement/Security, Piano/Expo, Servers/Tellers)
source.notes: Auto-generated captions with typical ASR noise (e.g. 'console office' for 'consulate office', 'Habana' for Hibana, 'Roto'/'Rotator' for rotate, 'H' for 'hard' etc. in a few spots). Creator uses a lot of informal/old-map callouts (e.g. 'Visa' vs 'Admin', 'Benches' vs 'West Corridor', 'Baguette' vs 'Secretary', 'Connector' vs 'Meeting Window') and flags his own confusion between some of them live in the video — both old and new names are kept in callouts where he uses both. One short aside (~12:35-14:00) covers attacking tips (opening caded hatches with Thermite/Hibana/Ace) which is explicitly NOT defense advice and has been excluded from this file's defense-focused arrays. Ash is mentioned once only as a description of common attacker behaviour (jumping through a window), not as a defense recommendation, and is excluded from operatorRecommendations/gadgetPlacements accordingly. Rewrite pass: no reliable top-down blueprint could be sourced for this rewrite (Chrome browser tooling for viewing the Ubisoft blueprint images was unavailable), so compass directions have been deliberately omitted from all location strings rather than guessed — locations use room/feature names only.

reinforcements.note: One of seven reinforcements on this site.

reinforcements.location: Consulate Office Wall into Meeting, Right Side
reinforcements.note: Covers the connector window as viewed from this side.
One of seven reinforcements on this site.
One of seven reinforcements on this site.
One of seven reinforcements on this site.

reinforcements.location: Break Room Wall into Managers, Right Side
reinforcements.note: One of seven reinforcements on this site.

reinforcements.location: Break Room Wall into Admin, Middle
reinforcements.note: One of seven reinforcements on this site.

headHoles.note: Watches Bathroom from the door. Watch for Yellow.
Second angle from the same door area.

headHoles.location: Left Hatch Above Small Box (into Piano)
headHoles.note: Shoot the left hatch only, not the middle one, so it doesn't break but still gives a line of sight down onto the door below. Hold it from below without fully breaking the hatch.

headHoles.location: Vertical Line onto CEO Window Plant Spot
headHoles.note: Small hole is enough to see someone planting behind the boot case. Use an impact grenade for a cleaner hole than a shotgun.
Holds Meeting/Connector Window, the breach door, Bathroom, the door to Long Desk, and Top Yellow from one position. Has a nearby cover spot.
Holds anyone coming out of Printer into Break Room. Fall back to a second set of head holes for a longer ACOG-range angle on the same door — SMG11 too inaccurate at this range.
Holds into Admin from the small box for anyone pushing through that door.

headHoles.location: Soft Wall near Breach, into Top of Spiral
headHoles.note: Gives an angle onto top of spiral. Better with an ACOG/Scope than an SMG11 at this range.
Lets you see through the breach opening to catch anyone tucking in close to it.

gadgetPlacements.location: Repel Area outside Connector Window Position
gadgetPlacements.note: Smoke this area to deny the window repel.

gadgetPlacements.location: Breach Doorway, C4 Launched Over the Top
gadgetPlacements.note: Lands near the default plant spot to deny it. Any C4 carrier.

generalTips.note: Presenter frequently confuses the 'Admin' (downstairs) and 'Visa' (upstairs) callouts.
High-ELO attackers generally push from Admin, take Admin/Break Room/Printer, then plant at the Connector Window position while holding across from two spots — hold or contest this area.
The room behind Break Room/Printer has no soft walls — if you hear a repel on the Printer/Copy window, retreat immediately.

Of the two Admin-facing windows, one angles through Break Room; the other is largely blocked by the reinforcement on this side. Some also reinforce the right side of Break for a peekable angle from both sides.
Don't hold this area all round — soak utility and kill drones for the first 60-90 seconds, then fall back without getting cut off from the stairs or flanked through Managers.
The Break Room machine/vending cover spot is a known anchor position at higher ELO — hard to dislodge without utility. Expect a shield plus ADS/gadgets soaking utility here.
When holding the small-box/Admin reinforcement position, play further back rather than against the wall — use the reinforcement as cover, hold briefly, then disengage.
After a breach opens, attackers usually drone left through it out of habit — hop onto the nearby table and tuck in near the breach to catch people who only check left.
From the top of the spiral stairs, attackers get sightlines toward Long Desk, Vending, and into Baguette (now officially Secretary).

reinforcements.note: First two reinforcements on this site.
Third reinforcement.
Fourth reinforcement.
Fifth reinforcement.
Sixth reinforcement.
Seventh reinforcement.

Eighth reinforcement — Option A. Lets you play Pillar and the long corridor from full cover, peeking the breach and corridor. Can't hold the breach from the Security Hall side; exposed to head holes from the breach if you go the wrong way.

Alternative eighth reinforcement — Option B, creator's preference. Pairs with head holes on this side; holds the breach and Yellow door from cover on the Security Hall side. Leaves an angle into the Pillar anchor spot if the hallway is left soft.
Ninth reinforcement. Protects the rotate itself and holds the security door from the head hole side.

headHoles.note: Paired with whichever side of the Pillar/Security wall is left soft.
Holds the security door. Small bit of cover plus the door to watch.
Get across between Cafe and Security with head holes covering the angle. Holds Yellow door, the breach, and peeks from Security Hallway.

rotates.note: One of the few rotates on this site.
Pair with head holes to get across and hold both sides.

gadgetPlacements.location: Default Camera Spot Upstairs, Corner of the Hatch
gadgetPlacements.note: Attackers commonly drone Visa, walk in and shoot the default cam right away. Place C4 in the corner near the hatch covering the default cam spot; detonate when they crouch to shoot the cam.
Attackers can stand on a book here to hold both the security hallway rotate and reinforced wall from one spot.

generalTips.note: Shoot the basement radio.
The main attacker win condition on this site is taking the Piano/Exposition vertical.
Leave the three walls near the hatch/vertical soft so they can be impacted/shotgunned later for surprise angles.
As a defender upstairs near the vertical, kill a few drones then get out — don't linger or you can be cut off from Visa-side windows.
If stuck below during a piano-side plant with no defenders left near the door, challenge the vertical directly.
Hold each room until pushed back rather than hunkering at the very back of site from the start.

reinforcements.note: First of nine reinforcements on this site (a tenth is optional).
Second reinforcement.
Third/fourth reinforcement.
Fifth/sixth reinforcement.

reinforcements.location: Expo Wall out to Benches (West Corridor) Window, Left Side
reinforcements.note: Optional/seventh reinforcement.

reinforcements.location: Main Breach Wall, out onto Balcony
reinforcements.note: Upstairs reinforcement.

reinforcements.location: CEO through to Meeting, Right Side
reinforcements.note: Upstairs reinforcement, mirrors the CEO/Meeting site's own setup.

reinforcements.location: Long-Corridor Wall near Piano Window Rotate
reinforcements.note: Optional reinforcement against a coordinated team; otherwise left soft for maneuverability.
Gives a line of sight for small box covering jump-ins, and cover for later-round pushes through this door.

headHoles.note: Optional, if the wall is left soft — long angle out onto mid/bottom-top spiral.

rotates.note: Can open the hatch alongside it, but you lose the rotate if you do.
Kept soft — lets you come out of either of two doors instead of getting trapped in a 1v2, and gives maneuverability to the piano window or into Lounge/Spiral.

gadgetPlacements.location: Meeting Double, Meeting Single, Top Yellow Door, Spiral-Side Door
gadgetPlacements.note: Barricade these spots when holding above a floor — cuts off multiple entry points, and any barricade being broken alerts you. Leave the hatch on your own side of the barricade. Alternative targets: Connector Window or CEO Window.
Line down onto the breach that goes to Lounge — a common push and plant corner. Make the hole closer to the breach to deny the angle to a crossing runner.
Watches pushes through the front door.
Hold the window from above without standing directly over it.

operatorRecommendations.note: Barricades multiple top-floor entries when holding above a floor.

generalTips.note: 'Benches' is the old callout for what's officially West Corridor.
Run two players upstairs — any decent attacking team will contest the vertical.
If trapped in a 1v2 downstairs with both attackers pushing Piano, hold this angle or the alternate one.
Because the front-door position is reinforced, you don't need to also reinforce the rotate wall into Lounge — play this room and challenge onto the front door, optionally with a deployable shield.
A good team will come upstairs and flush you out of the front-door hold — burn 3-4 of their drones first, don't get overly aggressive on the door.
Pre-fire when peeking the front door if the barricade is still closed.
Use the hatch on this site for mobility alongside the rotates.

reinforcements.note: First reinforcement on this site.

reinforcements.location: Construction into Cafe, Two End Walls
reinforcements.note: Second/third reinforcement.
Fourth reinforcement.
Fifth reinforcement.

reinforcements.location: Back of Towers through to Lounge, Two Walls
reinforcements.note: Sixth/seventh reinforcement.

reinforcements.location: Visa Corner Hatch, Opposite the Door
reinforcements.note: One of two hatches upstairs; the other is left soft.

headHoles.note: Site plays below with C4 denial above — hole at the door to hold it from below.
Only other way into Tellers besides the door — holds the window from below too.
Vertical holes either side of the desk (stand on the desk to make them) — sees anyone crouched on top, a common plant spot since C4 alone won't kill someone standing there.
Watch anyone coming through the double door; find the hatch, go left to locate the door.

rotates.note: No line of sight — for maneuverability around the site; without it you loop all the way around through the bottom of spiral.
Holds Servers and lets you move around to hold the other side.
Single wall (vs double) limits how attackers can hard-breach it, making a Mute deployment effective here.

hatches.location: Visa Corner Hatch, Opposite the Door

hatches.note: Left soft — opening it lets you drop down instead of running the stairs.
Left soft — gives a gunner-type player near the door/window/spiral a fast drop to safety after soaking utility.

gadgetPlacements.note: Deliberately left soft — not worth reinforcing.

gadgetPlacements.location: Tellers, High Up (not the back wall facing Visa)
gadgetPlacements.note: Avoid the back wall — it gets destroyed from Visa. Try a side wall, a dark corner, on top of the clock, or on the floor. Higher placement is better. Plant timer here is only 7 seconds.
Shoot an Ace charge off if someone starts placing one on the breach visible from this window.

operatorRecommendations.note: A single Jammer denies the Secret single wall — Thermite can't reopen it from just one side.
Near-essential cam pickup in Tellers to see the plant given the short 7-second plant timer. Echo drone/intel is an alternative source of the same information.

generalTips.note: One of the only sites where the bomb can be planted across two separate floors (Servers below, Tellers above) — requires full 5-stack coordination.
Worth holding Top Yellow and playing in the Break Room upstairs — holds the window and any vertical made against you from there too.
Playing upstairs near the door/window/spiral is for a gunner-type player who soaks 2-4 drones and some flashes/nades, then drops down to Servers via the soft hatch — avoid dying up there.
Shoot the basement radio.
If the two upstairs reinforcements aren't used there, put them on the breach or along the Cafe walls; soak utility on 2-3 drones pushing Cafe, then fall back behind those reinforcements.
Defend each room until pushed back rather than hunkering directly on the plant spot from round start.
Burn attackers' drones and utility before they can execute.
